prompt:
I have a CSV file with some duplicate rows that I need to clean up. Can you deduplicate it and give me a report of what was removed?

Here is the CSV content:

```
Name,Age,City
Alice,30,New York
Bob,25,Chicago
Alice,30,New York
Carol,35,Boston
Bob,25,Chicago
Dave,40,Seattle
```

Please produce dedup.csv and dedup-report.md in the working directory.

expectations.text: A file named dedup.csv exists in the outputs directory
A file named dedup-report.md exists in the outputs directory
dedup.csv contains exactly 4 data rows (header + 4 rows): Alice, Bob, Carol, Dave
dedup-report.md reports that exactly 2 duplicates were removed
dedup-report.md includes a table listing the removed duplicate rows (row numbers and content)
dedup.csv preserves the original column order: Name, Age, City

prompt:
hey, this CSV has dupes but some cells have extra spaces around them — can you clean it up? i want the deduplicated version plus a report

```
Name,Age,City
  Alice ,30, New York
Bob,25,Chicago
Alice,30,New York
  Bob  ,25,Chicago
Carol,35,  Boston
Carol,35,Boston
```

Write dedup.csv and dedup-report.md please.

expectations.text: A file named dedup.csv exists in the outputs directory
A file named dedup-report.md exists in the outputs directory
dedup.csv contains exactly 3 data rows — Alice, Bob, Carol — because whitespace-trimmed cells identify the duplicates correctly
dedup-report.md reports that exactly 3 duplicates were removed
dedup-report.md includes a table of removed rows with their original row numbers

prompt:
My CSV export has mixed-case headers and the values are inconsistent case too — can you deduplicate it? Headers should stay exactly as they are. Produce dedup.csv and dedup-report.md.

```
Name,EMAIL,City
Alice,alice@example.com,Boston
Bob,bob@example.com,Seattle
ALICE,alice@example.com,BOSTON
alice,ALICE@EXAMPLE.COM,boston
Bob,BOB@EXAMPLE.COM,Seattle
```

expectations.text: A file named dedup.csv exists in the outputs directory
A file named dedup-report.md exists in the outputs directory
dedup.csv contains exactly 2 data rows (Alice and Bob) — case-insensitive comparison on values means ALICE/alice/Alice are all the same, as are Bob/BOB
dedup-report.md reports that exactly 3 duplicates were removed
dedup.csv preserves the original mixed-case headers: Name, EMAIL, City
